distillationMethod.outputDecision: single integrated skill because modeling, logic mapping, graph operations, CGIF interchange, and audit checks are mutually dependent

findingsBeforeRepair.summary: Execution steps had completion checks, but no consolidated machine-checkable gate protocol or explicit recovery action when checks failed.
The skill carried source anchors but did not explicitly state that ordinary execution must not depend on reading the original source book.
Test prompts covered major trigger classes but did not strongly verify standalone modeling, negative-context canonical operation direction, quantifier-scope ambiguity, or CGIF repair behavior.

repairsApplied: Added a Standalone Contract section that treats source files as traceability anchors rather than runtime prerequisites.
Added a Gate protocol section covering output contract, proposition coverage, signatures, coreference, contexts, target fit, and audit handling.
Converted step completion checks into explicit gates.

Added Failure Modes and Recovery for missing targets, ambiguous scope, unknown signatures, broken labels, identity collisions, unsupported target features, incomparable graphs, and visual-only requests.
Added a unified output format for modeling, translation, audit, and comparison tasks.

Expanded test prompts to cover standalone execution, canonical operations, should-not-trigger graph database/styling prompts, CGIF repair, quantifier scope, negative contexts, and ISO-only modality edge cases.
remainingRisks: CGIF examples are method-oriented and not parser-verified against a concrete CGIF implementation.
Rich belief, desire, modality, indexical, Proposition, and Situation contexts may still require approximate or extended treatment depending on the target Common Logic dialect.
Local relation signatures remain task-specific assumptions unless the user supplies an ontology or type hierarchy.

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.
